entry.title: Re: Currency other than official for a localized country

Issue with all the apps both in BAS and VS tool for migrating the apps from the neo web ide.

Do any one have the solution how to resolve this.

entry.title: i need to block the upload and creation of sap FSM if the related BP in SAP Business One is inactive
entry.content: I need to block the modification and creation of activities and service calls in SAP FSM if the related business partner in SAP Business One is inactive.

entry.title: SAP Jobs & Internships in Armenia
entry.content:
Looking for your next big move? Or got an open role to share?
This is the space to post and discover job opportunities, internships, and freelance gigs within our community. Let’s help each other grow 🚀

entry.title: CVE-2024-4741 (OpenSSL) vulnerability in Crystal Reports Runtime for VS
entry.content:
Could you please confirm plans to address CVE-2024-4741 (OpenSSL) in Crystal Reports Runtime for VS?

SP37 contains libssl-3-x64.dll version 3.2.0.0. It needs to be 3.2.2 or later to address this vulnerability.

entry.title: Re: Joule Setup / Error during Booster Execution
entry.content: Hi George, we had instances on the respective subaccounts. We also thought that this may be the cause of the error, so i changed it today and used one subaccount for joule and buildworkzone. Now the formation seems to have been created without errors. We still have some other errors but for now Joule is working.

entry.title: Access to practise environment SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Hi, I just bought the LearningHub lizence and started THR80. I now need to get access to the practise environment but do not understand how to get there. Could someone help me?

Best regards

Ralf

entry.content:
Alliance for Recruitment is currently looking for HR IT Payroll Specialist from Armenia who will work with SAP HCM Payroll and other HR business applications with gross monthly salary of 4000-6000 Eur. 

Let me know if you are a good fit!

https://www.afr.lt/lt/job-offers/hr-payroll-specialist

entry.title: Re: SAP Jobs & Internships in Armenia
entry.content:
Ttwo-month internships at ACBA

ACBA Bank / RevyTech  is currently accepting  applications to internship@acba.am with the subject line "For SAP Business Consulting" and include your CV.

Applicants are advised to complete the Learning Journeys listed below. The more courses completed, the higher the chances of selection!

1.Discovering End-to-End Business Processes for the Intelligent Enterprise

2.Exploring Cloud ERP 

1.Implementing SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

entry.title: S/4 Hana Public Cloud - Workforce Management Perspective
entry.content:
To Begin , this article is for people who are planning to implement S/4HANA Public cloud along with Employee Central as the frontend for HR data.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition is a ready-to-run enterprise resource planning (ERP) system that delivers the latest industry best practices and business application solutions on a public cloud network. It offers an efficient, agile, and affordable solution for connecting your workforce, suppliers, customers, markets, and assets without adding complexity. 

 

HR in S/4 HANA Public cloud

 

For HR processes, SAP recommends using SAP SuccessFactors solutions, which are seamlessly integrated with SAP S/4HANA Cloud. In S/4 HANA cloud employees are termed as workforce persons and process of managing their data is referred as workforce management.

 

HR information is processed and stored in HR system. However, basic employee information is required to execute various processes in S/4 system such as project planning, billing, project time recording etc. This information is made available through workforce integration

 

SAP SuccessFactors system can be integrated with S/4 system through Master Data Integration covered in scope ID JB1 Core HR with SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central (JB1) . This scope supports transfer of employee data (including contingent workers). It includes basic data, such as employee name and ID, and contact details, such as workplace address, e-mail, and telephone information.

 

Below are relevant scope ids where integrated HR data is used

J12- Time Recording - Project-Based Services (J12)

6JO- Resource Management for Projects (6JO)

Integration (MDI)

SAP Master Data Integration service is a central master data hub. Applications integrate with SAP Master Data Integration to synchronize their local master data databases with the master data database of the central hub.

Data flow and field mapping

SAP Master Data Integration enables you to share consistent master data across multiple products easily and efficiently. Once the relevant products, applications, or landscapes are connected to SAP Master Data Integration, master data is synchronized between them.

Following order for data flow is recommended for successful data replication between S/4 HANA Public cloud and SAP SuccessFactors.

Step

Entity

Source

Destination

1

Company Code

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

SAP SuccessFactors

2

Cost Center

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

SAP SuccessFactors

3

Bank Data

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

SAP SuccessFactors

4

Workforce person

SAP SuccessFactors

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

5

Availability information

SAP SuccessFactors

S/4 HANA Public Cloud

Replication of master data such as Company code, cost center and bank data to SuccessFactors determines base to process employee information and maintain data which will be uniform once it is replicated back to S/4 HANA Public cloud.

Field Mapping between S/4 HANA Public Cloud and SuccessFactors

SAP has provided pre-defined field mappings for most of the entities when Employee master data is replicated from SuccessFactors to S/4 HANA. SAP note 3078790 provides a list of mapping and configuration activities required in case of custom data handling.

Integration Process

 

We will cover end to end integration process from both S/4 and SF systems. The process of configuring integration should follow below sequence. These steps are described in detail.

 

 

S.No.

Step

In which system to perform the step

1.

Creating a service instance and a service key

SAP Business Technology Platform

2.

Configure a business system ID

SAP Business Technology Platform

3.

Configure write permissions

SAP Business Technology Platform

4.

Create user in SF for communication

SAP SuccessFactors

5.

Creating communication systems and communication arrangements

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

6.

Configuring Destinations

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition

7.

Configure distribution models in Business Data Orchestration

SAP Business Technology Platform

8.

Configure OAuth connection

SAP SuccessFactors

9.

Configuration for Master Data Integration for all object types

SAP SuccessFactors

10.

Enable Address Mapping

SAP SuccessFactors

11.

Configure Business Scenario

SAP SuccessFactors

12.

Configure Availability Iflow

SAP Integration Suite

 

 

  1. Creating a service instance and a service key

For each application you connect, you will have to create a dedicated service instance of SAP Master Data Integration from the SAP BTP marketplace. After creating the service instance, you create a service binding to obtain the credentials to establish the technical connectivity to the application.

 

  • Navigate to Services > Service Marketplace and select Master Data Integration. Choose Create and enter information required.
  • Service - select Master Data Integration
  • Service plan- select sap-integration. This service plan must be chosen for connecting SAP-branded cloud applications (e.g., SAP S/4HANA Cloud).
  • Provide instance name
  • In the next screen configure service instance parameters. Provide a value for the mandatory application attribute based on the application that you intend to connect. We have maintained “sfsf” as provided by SAP for SAP SuccessFactors Application (ref. Connecting Applications via Service Instances | SAP Help Portal )

2.Configure a business system ID and write permissions

The businessSystemId acts as a display name for the client represented by the service instance in the Business Data Orchestration UI. Also, the businessSystemId configuration attribute is mandatory to connect a client using the SOAP API for Business Partner.

 

You should only configure businessSystemId if the documentation of the connected application demands it.

 

  • Navigate to the service instance.
  • Select ... -> View Parameters to view the current attribute configuration of the service instance.
  • Copy the JSON object with the current state of attributes.
  • Select ... -> Update. Paste the existing JSON object and add, update, or remove the relevant attributes. Note that the update operation does not patch the existing configuration, it replaces the full state of configuration. Therefore, it is important to provide all relevant attributes in the update operation.
  • Add the following attribute to configure the businessSystemId.

 

"businessSystemId": "<businessSystemId>"

 

  • The businessSystemId attribute should have a length of up to 60 characters and it must be unique within a tenant.
  • Add the following attribute to configure the writePermissions

 

"writePermissions": [

                                                            { "entityType": "<entityTypeOdmName1>" },

                                                            { "entityType": "<entityTypeOdmName2>" },

                                                            ...

                                                            ]

Example-

"writePermissions": [

                                             { "entityType": "sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter" },

                                             ]

 

 

3.Creating communication systems

  • Navigate to Communication Systems app. Create New system
  • Define ID and name
  • Enter General and contact details
  • Host Name- Enter the host name of the remote system for outgoing API service calls. Please note that the usage of localhost is not allowed as host name.
  • Enter users for inbound and outbound communication.

 

 

4.Create communication arrangements

Create below communication arrangements to allow data replication of our required objects:

  • SAP_COM_0001 Employee Integration
  • SAP_COM_0644 Photo Replication
  • SAP_COM_0056 Employee Central Cost Center Integration
  • SAP_COM_0379 Bank - Bank Integration
  • SAP_COM_0441 Employee Central Financial Master Data Integration
  • SAP_COM_0914 Workforce Availability Integration
  • SAP_COM_0301External HR Workforce Integration                                        
  • SAP_COM_0594Business Data Orchestration Integration                             
  • SAP_COM_0659SAP Master Data Integration                                                   
  • SAP_COM_0943Business Object Cost Center Integration

5.Configuring Destinations

These steps are required to set up a destination for the connection to Business Data Orchestration

  • Go to your SAP BTP Cockpit under Subaccount  Connectivity  Destinations.
  • Provide below details to create new destination:
  • Name
  • Type- HTTP
  • URL- Provide the service URL from the MDO Distribution Administration OData V4 service. Look for MDO: Distribution Administration among the inbound services with application protocol OData V4 that was generated while creating the communication arrangement SAP_COM_0594, and copy the entire URL into this field.
  • Create different distribution model for each object type to be replicated with URL specified for each object type.
  • User and Password: As defined for the user assigned to the communication system

 

Workforce data

URL- https://my<number>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/mdo_distributionadmin/srvd_a2x/sap/distributionadmin/0001/

 

Bank Data

URL- https://my<number>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_bank/srvd_a2x/sap/bank/0003/Bank

 

Cost center

URL- https://my<number>-api.s4hana.cloud.sap/sap/opu/odata4/sap/api_cost_center/srvd_a2x/sap/costcenter/0001/A_CostCenterText_2

 

 

 

 

6.Configure distribution models in Business Data Orchestration

Create separate distribution models for each object type with details as below:

  • Model    name- Customer specific name for each model
  • Business Object Type- Respective business object type for each object type
    • Workforce Person- sap.odm.workforce.WorkforcePerson
    • Bank Data- sap.odm.finance.bank.Bank
    • Cost center- sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter
  • Mode- Mode of data flow
    • Workforce Person- Pull
    • Bank Data- Push
    • Cost Center- Create 2 distribution models one with Push mode and one with Pull mode
  • Provider interface- choose respective API with latest version
    • Workforce Person- MDI_LOG_WORKFORCE_PERSON
    • Bank Data- MDI_LOG_BANK
    • Cost Center- MDI_LOG_COST_CENTER(Pull)/ MDI_CHANGE_COST_CENTER(Push)
  • Consumer- Choose a consumer destination for the distribution model created in previous section.

 

 

 

7.Configure OAuth connection

  •           Go to Admin Center > Security Center > OAuth Configurations
  •              Add new configuration
  •              Grant Type: Client_Credentials
  •              Client ID: Value from clientid in the service key credentials
  •              Client Secret: Value from clientsecret in the service key credentials
  •              Token URL: Value from url in the service key credentials + /oauth/token

 

8.Configuration for Master Data Integration for all object types

  • Go to Manage Data> Create new > SAP Master Data Integration Configuration
  • Select Integration object as Bank
  • Select Parameter as “Is Migration object”
  • Provide response value as No. Save the configuration.
  • Repeat new configuration for integration object as Workforce Person.
  • Select and add parameter for photo replication if photo replication is required.
  • Provide cutoff dates by selecting relevant parameters.

 

9.Enable Address Mapping

  • Go to SAP SuccessFactors Upgrade Center and search for Best Practices Address Mapping for SAP Master Data Integration.
  • Select the option and choose Upgrade Now. Address mapping is configured automatically.

 

 

10.Configure Business Scenarios

  • You would need to run/schedule certain business scenarios from EC in order to trigger master data replication. These can be found under Admin Center> Business Scenarios
  • Bank/ Cost Center Replication from SAP Master Data Integration to EC – You need to provide Oauth config , API URL in source settings
  • Provide operation URI specific for object type-
  • Bank- /v0/odm/3.0.0/log/sap.odm.finance.bank.Bank
  • Cost center- /v0/odm/3.1.0/log/sap.odm.finance.costobject.CostCenter

 

  • For Workforce Replication- Provide details in destination settings. Set HTTP Method as POST
  •  

  •  

  •  

 

 

 

 

11.Configure Availability Iflow

 

  •    Select integration package and artifact

Select integration package as “SAP SuccessFactors - Employee Availability Integration with SAP S/4HANA Cloud” and artifact as “SFSF_EC_to_S4HANA_Availability”

  •    Configuration

Go to Security material under Manage security section and maintain the user and passwords for S/4 HANA and SuccessFactors systems.

 

In the artifact below screen appears. Click on configuration and set configuration values for the integration.

 

  • Set schedule of replication as required

 

 

 

Business Partner and Vendor creation

 

A work assignment defines the relationship between a worker and a specific company or legal entity, also known as a work agreement. Each work assignment is uniquely identified by a Work Assignment ID.

 

A workforce person can have multiple work assignments, either at different times or simultaneously. When there are multiple assignments, the system automatically determines the main employment, which indicates the current active employment.

 

Each workforce person and their work assignments are represented by various business partners, each with a specific role.

 

Points to note-

  • To represent an internal workforce person or employee, one BP with the Employee role is created.
  • For each work assignment for an employee, a separate BP with the Employment role is created.
  • Bank data is stored on the employment BP. If bank data is maintained in employment BP, the Supplier (FLVN00) role is added to employment BP automatically.

 

Conclusion :- This Article gives you Indepth insight of the workfoce managment and integration between successfactors to S4/Hana Public cloud. 

In the next Blog , the aspects of integration with Resource management will be covered including talent intelligence hub and availability control from EC to Resource management.

Thanks to Divya Gopal , Amruta Chitale , Arghya Sengupta , Mahesh Joshi  , Nitin Patil , Sourabh Panigrahi , Mayank Barui from My current Organization to help and contribute to this Blog.

entry.title: Re: Adding Quote Items to Existing Sales Order in ECC
entry.content:
@VasanthaSAPCPQ 

As you mentioned SAP ECC, its purely you will have to analyze the need from customer and to be developed as custom solutions. There is no standard solutions for your question. you can use the APIs to consolidate and have it into Sales Order..

entry.title: Re: SAC Story date Issue
entry.content:
@yoursraghu986 

So If I understand this correctly, With Date IP control you have managed to show the column is red but you also want to show the yellow column which is January month Opening Balance. You can try the below with some scripting.

  • Create a restricted measure calculation for the column in yellow. Use Input Control for restricting Date.
  • Now you can use script to read the current Input control selection which will return 2024.10, Use script to manipulate this to create 2024.01 ( 01 is fixed, you just need to get year from date ip control selection). Store this in variable. Alternatively you can also read this from table if date member is available in axis or filter.
  • Use this value to set the input control of restricted measure.

Useful API's

InputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers()

InputControlDataSource().setSelectedMembers()

 

Hope this helps !!

Nikhil

entry.title: The Next Decade of Enterprise Technology: Are We Ready?
entry.content:
The world of enterprise technology is on the brink of an unprecedented transformation. From AI-driven automation to low-code development and cloud-based ecosystems, the landscape is shifting rapidly. Organizations that fail to adapt risk falling behind, while those that embrace innovation will gain a competitive edge. I want to talk about the three major trends that are fueling that transformation.

 

The Three Major Trends Shaping Enterprise Technology



1. AI & Automation as a Core Business Strategy

Artificial intelligence (AI) is no longer a futuristic concept—it is the backbone of modern enterprises. Organizations that successfully integrate AI into their business models will not only streamline operations but also unlock new revenue streams. AI-powered tools like SAP Joule bring generative AI into enterprise workflows, enabling predictive analytics, automation, and intelligent decision-making at scale.

Automation is also shifting from simple robotic process automation (RPA) to intelligent process automation (IPA). Unlike traditional automation, IPA incorporates machine learning, cognitive intelligence, and data analytics, allowing businesses to automate more complex tasks. This shift means companies need to think beyond cost reduction and focus on value creation through automation.

2. The Consumerization of Enterprise Software

The workforce of today expects the same seamless, user-friendly experience from enterprise software as they do from consumer apps. The traditional complexity of enterprise software is becoming a barrier to efficiency, and organizations are increasingly investing in modern, intuitive solutions.

SAP Fiori is a prime example of how enterprise software is evolving. By providing a streamlined, easy-to-navigate UI, SAP Fiori enhances productivity and user adoption. Similarly, low-code and no-code platforms like SAP Build empower employees across departments to create applications and automate processes without extensive programming knowledge. This democratization of development is accelerating innovation across industries. Additionally, adding WalkMe’s smart usability capabilities and expanding AI functionalities adds a layer of significant user-experience enhancement.

3. From ERP to Connected Ecosystems

For decades, enterprises relied on monolithic ERP systems to manage their core processes. However, the future lies in composable architectures and interconnected ecosystems to allow for fast adaptation to changes and reduced time to shipping new products and processes. In order to secure that speed and flexibility, organizations will move towards products like the revolutionary SAP Business Data Cloud, which allows them to unify and analyze data from multiple sources in real time, breaking down traditional data silos and providing access to cross-silo data for training of Large Language Models and the use of agentic AI platforms like Joule.

By leveraging cloud platforms, APIs, and microservices, businesses can now create more flexible, scalable IT infrastructures that support innovation and adaptability.

Preparing for the Next Decade

To stay ahead of the curve, enterprises must adopt a proactive technology strategy. This involves:


✅ Investing in AI and automation to enhance productivity and decision-making.
✅ Focusing on user experience to improve software adoption and efficiency.
✅ Embracing connected ecosystems for real-time data analysis and insights.
✅ Prioritizing security and compliance to protect sensitive business data in an increasingly digital world.

The question is no longer whether businesses should adopt these technologies, but how quickly they can implement them to remain competitive.

What do you think is the most important enterprise tech trend shaping the next decade?

entry.title: Re: SAP Opens Restaurant
entry.content: Went there. Mi initial beer was empty. As expected.

entry.title: Re: Restrict the "Release" and "Pay" button in the App "Make Bank transfers

entry.title: Data Access Control in Data Builder and Authorization Scenario in Business Builder - SAP Datasphere.
entry.content:
Hello All,

Welcome back again. Before exploring this blog post, kindly visit the below link, where I created a scenario and demo on DAC based on hierarchy 

Link :  https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/data-access-control-in-data-builder-

and-authorization-scenario-in-business/ba-p/14063171 


Now let us explore DAC along with the new authorization scenario that is available in Business Builder. This blogpost is going to be a unique post where I have illustrated a business case exploring both DAC in the Data Builder and the New Authorization Scenario in Business Builder for the business folks.

Now let us deep dive and understand the scenario. Here in this blog post I am not going to discuss the theory part, as there are enough sources available on the internet. We will understand the DAC and new authorization scenario both with an example. Now get set and go!

1st Step :

Create a product dimension table.

Business Name: Products_Dim

Technical Name: Products_Dim
Below are the table attributes and records.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
So our dimension table is ready.
 
 
Step 2 : Create the control table or permission table or Authorization table.
 
below are the bsuiness name and technical name given to the permission table.
 
Business Name : Authorization_Example.
Technical Name: Authorization_Example

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

Here in this permission table we have maintained 2 users. user sapbtp00 has access to both the product IDs and user sapbtp03 has access to only 1 product ID. Now in next steps let us create a DAC and apply that DAC to a view on products_dim table.

Step 3: Now create a DAC, i.e., Data access control

Now we have created Data access control and apply this DAC to the view.

Step 4: Apply the DAC created to the view.

 

Now view the data in this model.

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

As user sapbtp00 is viewing the data in the model, he has access to see only 2 products in the permission table that is why he is seeing 2 records, but when sapbtp03 person will run the view then he can only see the product RO-1001 only. you can test the same.
 
Now our DAC is ready in the Data Builder area. Now let us move to Business Builder area to create the new authorization scenario.
 
Business Builder in SAP Datasphere.
 
To give a short introduction, SAP clearly wanted to bifurcate between data engineers, as mainly their scope is in data builder, and for business people SAP created a business builder app where the business people can create their entity as per their need and play with the model. This reduces the gap between data engineers and business people and helps to maintain a balance and sync between both parties. with this business folks can create their model be it ER models or analytical models, in the business builder app and also create authorization scenarios. They can also create ADHOC storytelling dashboards as per their need. 
 
Now let us go to Business Builder and create new authorization scenario based on the DAC we created in data builder and product dimension table.
 
Now again get set go!
 

 

 
Before creating new authorization scenarios now, let us create dimensions and make sure business builder can consume those.
 
So let us create a dimension in business builder.
 
Step 1: Create a dimension.
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 
 

 

 

the dataset in the dimension in Business Builder.

 

so the dimension is ready in Business Builder.

Step 2:

Now, let us create a new authorization scenario.

 so we created a new authorization scenario based on the DAC we created in Data Builder.

Step 3: Apply the new authorization scenario to the products Dim table.

Now we created a new authorization scenario and applied the new auth scenario to the products dim table in the business builder app. Now we can view the data in the dim table.

 

Step 4 : Save this dimension as a consumption model.

 

so we created a consumption model as well. Perfect. But in a real project, it is ideal to create a consumption model based on a fact, as FACT is a multidimensional model with multiple associations.

Now let us see if this consumption model is visible to SAC or not. we will need to create a new perspective for the same. Now let us go to SAC.

in SAC we can see the perspective of the consumption model.

 

We can consume this model and create a story in SAC and enjoy it.

the above example illustrates the link between data builder and business builder with DAC in data builder and New Authorization scenario in business builder.

Thank you in advance.

Narasingha.

entry.title: Zero Delivery OBD still showing in Open for Billing

Regards

 

Sanjay Arora

entry.title: Re: Early billing in Phased Contract in BRIM

entry.title: Billing document -PDF Extract
entry.content: We would like to explore if there is a way to mass download invoices PDF format potentially thousand at a time. Currently we do not have an API available for this process. Could you please suggest a suitable approach.

entry.title: Record Warehouse defect serial managed material
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

As described on this SAP Help site to perform a posting in EWM with a quality notification it is required to create and link a warehouse defect in advance to provide to stock-specific information for the quality notification.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_ON-PREMISE/2bc3ee8d1c83404e8cf62418640004f2/15a64024397f4e219c2c800576023304.html?locale=en-US

Unfortunately the standard fiori app doesn't support serialized products. Already discussed with a SAP product/topic expert:

https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-q-a/fiori-apps-quot-record-warehouse-defect-quot-does-not-support-product-with/qaq-p/12685370

This topic is also not mentioned in the SAP Roadmap.

Is there any way to return a serialized material in private cloud to a supplier with a quality notfication process?

Kind regards

Michael

entry.title: Object Definition not coming in Manage Data
entry.content: I have created a custom Object definition but it is not available in manage Data.

entry.title: Re: Dashboard Usage Reporting
entry.content:
Hi Adam,

If you already have enabled Story Reports then you can import a template from the content store that shows report execution of table reports and Tiles & Dashboards: https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_SUCCESSFACTORS_PEOPLE_ANALYTICS/1a17ad0982664c629f8af7d47a631672/15c797546af74165b7489ad35e844c5d.html?locale=en-US

In case you have not yet enabled Story Reports then please read this IDP document for creating such a report in Table or Canvas reports: https://d.dam.sap.com/a/QN2myUU/SAP%20SuccessFactors%20People%20Analytics%20Story%20ReportV1.8-Dec2024.pdf?rc=67&inline=true

Kind regards,

Jeroen

entry.title: Add f4 help to field CUST1 in ROIITANKDIP structure in SAP
entry.content: Need to add f4 help using standard search help for field CUST1 of structure ROIITANKDIP in sap

entry.title: Request for SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance Fiori Demo Videos & Resources
entry.content:
Hi everyone, I need to demonstrate key SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance processes using the Fiori Demo environment, and we already have access to the SAP Demo system. Could anyone help by sharing reference videos, online resources, or YouTube links that include data models and step-by-step demonstrations? Any insights, links, or references to hands-on demonstrations would be highly appreciated! Specifically, we are looking for Fiori demo videos covering the following topics:

L4 Business Process Topics (BPD Topics):

  • Phase In / Phase Out of Equipment
  • Non-Routine Maintenance (Reactive, Minor, Emergency Maintenance)
  • Routine/Preventive Maintenance
  • Refurbishment (In-House / External)
  • Condition-Based Maintenance
  • Maintenance Planning
  • Resource Scheduling Management
  • Shutdown Maintenance
  • Routine Maintenance (Work Clearance Management)                                                                            

    Thanks in advance.

entry.title: IFRS 15 - Revenue Recognition
entry.content:
Hi, 

We'r in a public cloud implementation and the customer is reporting related to IFRS 15, I found in Cloud ALM that there is a requirment to raise a ticket if this is needed. In Cloud ALM and when reading about this there is one reference to a note 2524569 in several places however this not is not available and haven't been for a while. Where can I find this information instead? Any tips? 

Kind Regards, Sanne

entry.title: Re: Sub-consolidation with Minimal Cons Groups
entry.content:
Hello Eyal,

If your sub consolidation (for consolidation unit 3) does not include group dependent entries (posting levels 02,12,22,30) you can run a group report with selecting a consolidation hierarchy and no consolidation group (select #).

This will select the data of the units included in the hierarchy nodes, and the eliminations (posting level 20) posted between consolidation units and partner units included in the hierarchy nodes. The eliminations will be displayed on elimination members (one per hierarchy node).

This is described here: 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/90c07e91c7a64f328be3fd6b48955b13/86b7e4024e4b4264891ce4d377ca075c.html?locale=en-US

Hope this helps!

Best regards,

Grégoire

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori tools extensions pack issue for migrating neo apps from web ide
entry.content: Already posted yesterday https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-q-a/bas-application-modeler-broken-with-fiori-tools-1-17-1/qaq-p/14063117

entry.title: Re: What’s New in SAP HANA Cloud – December 2024
entry.content: Hi @thomashammer, does HANA have support for full text searching in JSON Document Stores, to search through fields in JSON documents, the way that you can with Oracle: Introducing JSON Full-Text Search with Oracle Globally Distributed Database 23ai ?

entry.title: Workaround to category page limitations in UDM
entry.content:
The issue

Featured jobs component in conjunction with category pages no longer displays results inside a job layout.

Let's assume we have the category page "HR" with the rule below:

 

Now I connect the category page inside a "Featured jobs" component in a job layout:

 

If I click on "HR", initially I will get the "unfiltered" list of jobs:

 

Once I refresh the page, only the filtered result will display:

The Workaround

Start by creating an active landing page

Copy the link from the landing page and write it down 🙂

Return to your job layout and, inside the featured jobs component, connect the link of the landing page with link type = external.

Make sure to deactivate "open link in new session" which is set as default, to ensure a better candidate experience.

Return to your category page.

The category page should be created using simple rules (no Solr syntax is supported in UDM). Copy the link.

 

Now return to your landing page.

Inside of the landing page, add a custom plugin.

 

Click on the custom plugin and add the following code:

<embed src="link from your category page" style="width:500px; height: 300px;">

Click "Publish".

Search for a job matching the layout where you added the featured jobs component.

 

Click on it.

 

You will notice that the jobs are now filtered. The whole purpose of this workaround is to "force" the system to trigger a page refresh instead of the candidate.

You will have to redo these steps for all categories.

entry.title: Tax on cash discount during payment F110
entry.content: I have created invoice with both GST and withholding tax, when cleard the invoice using F110 I can see cash discount line items are split and tax calculated for both the line items in clearing document. Is this expected behaviour? If this is wrong how do I correct it?

entry.title: Collections Process receivables Open Amount 0.00
entry.content:
Open Amount is 0.00 in the App Process receivables for open collection items.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Collections Process receivables Open Amount 0.00
entry.content:
This is the standard design of the product that the open amount will always be 0.00, if a payment method is maintained for the posting - The amount to be collected directly corresponds with the open amount, as if there is a payment method, the payment is arranged and doesn't need to be collected by the collections specialists.

Please see the details in SAP KBA https://me.sap.com/notes/3591068

entry.title: Re: A company to multiple sales area error
entry.content:
Hello,

Problem: Cannot Assign Plant to Sales Area

Solution

  • You need to configure the sales area association properly in the system. Use the SSCUI configuration step ID: 102287 to assign the sales organization, distribution channel, and plant relationship.

Steps

  1. Open Manage Your Solution App. 2742460 - How to Assign a Plant to a Sales Organisation in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  2. Open Configure Your Solution.
  3. Search for SSCUI 102287.
  4. In step “Assign sales organization - distribution channel - plant”, add the necessary entries for Sales Organization 1310, Distribution Channel E, and Plant 1310.

Problem: Error in Customer Master Data

Solution

Ensure proper configuration of sales organization and plant relationships in Table TVKWZ. The values you want to use must exist in this configuration table before assignment.

Problem: Product Master Data Issues

Solution

Similar to the previous steps, ensure plant assignments are properly maintained in the product distribution chains using the SSCUI for assigning plants to sales areas.

Problem: Sales Order Creation Error

Solution

The issue is typically caused by missing configuration linking the sales organization, distribution channel, and plant. Again, you need to ensure these are configured correctly in the step “Assign sales organization - distribution channel - plant” 2941039 - Error ‘Sales from sales organization &, dist. chan.&, plant & are not defined’ when creating a sales order in ‘Create Sales Orders - VA01’ app - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with SSCUI ID: 102287.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: 'Created Date' within the 'Captured 1:1 Meeting' (CPM) schema.
entry.content: Hi! And what is the problem now? As far as I understood if you include only User ID and Created date, you have the data (1 row per person). If they did 2-3 comments per day, do you want it to appear once or the same date to appear 3 times?

entry.title: Re: CDS的字段值显示问题
entry.content:
Hello,

When adding I_SalesDocumentItem._ShippingPoint._Text.ShippingPointName to the CDS view, you have found that the data is repeated, showing multiple entries instead of a single entry for each shipping point.

Why It Occurs

The duplication happens because the ShippingPointName might be maintained in multiple languages (e.g., Chinese and English). This leads to multiple entries for the same shipping point based on its names in different languages.

Possible Solutions

  1. Filter By Language: Apply a filter to show names only in one specific language. You can filter the data by adding a condition that specifies the language code you want, like ‘EN’ for English, in the CDS view definition.

  2. Aggregation with Grouping: Use aggregation functions and GROUP BY clause to pick the first entry of each shipping point name, effectively ignoring the duplicates.

  3. Custom Logic for Language Preference: Implement custom logic using ABAP or a BADI to set preferred language for text descriptions, which will automatically select the proper language version.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: SAP HANA Flowgraphs: What SQL Statements are executed?
entry.content:
Hi,

We have some more complex flowgraphs and think about to move them to procedures. But, we would like to know, what SQL statements is being executed. Do you have any idea to get them with easy effort?

Thank you.

entry.title: com/sap/cloud/mt/subscription/exits/SubscribeExit.class file not found after CDS Feb 2025 upgrade
entry.content:
Hi team,

I have upgraded my java backend application to CDS February 2025 version. 

I am using 3.8.0 cds version inside pom.xml.

However, I am getting following error after the upgrade when deploying my application with cf push:
 
org.springframework.beans.factory.BeanDefinitionStoreException: I/O failure while processing configuration class [com.sap.xyz.v2.lib.manageddb.exits.SubscribeExit]
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:251)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.parse(ConfigurationClassParser.java:189)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.doProcessConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:314)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:246)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.parse(ConfigurationClassParser.java:197)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.parse(ConfigurationClassParser.java:165)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.processConfigBeanDefinitions(ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.java:417)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.postProcessBeanDefinitionRegistry(ConfigurationClassPostProcessor.java:290)
org.springframework.context.support.PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.invokeBeanDefinitionRegistryPostProcessors(PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.java:349)
org.springframework.context.support.PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.invokeBeanFactoryPostProcessors(PostProcessorRegistrationDelegate.java:118)
org.springframework.context.support.AbstractApplicationContext.invokeBeanFactoryPostProcessors(AbstractApplicationContext.java:789)
org.springframework.context.support.AbstractApplicationContext.refresh(AbstractApplicationContext.java:607)
org.springframework.boot.web.servlet.context.ServletWebServerApplicationContext.refresh(ServletWebServerApplicationContext.java:146)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.refresh(SpringApplication.java:754)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.refreshContext(SpringApplication.java:456)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:335)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:1363)
org.springframework.boot.SpringApplication.run(SpringApplication.java:1352)
com.sap.xyz.v2.renn.bpp.BPApplication.main(BPApplication.java:12)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)
java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(Unknown Source)
java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Unknown Source)
org.springframework.boot.loader.launch.Launcher.launch(Launcher.java:91)
org.springframework.boot.loader.launch.Launcher.launch(Launcher.java:53)
org.springframework.boot.loader.launch.JarLauncher.main(JarLauncher.java:58)
Caused by: java.io.FileNotFoundException: class path resource [com/sap/cloud/mt/subscription/exits/SubscribeExit.class] cannot be opened because it does not exist
org.springframework.core.io.ClassPathResource.getInputStream(ClassPathResource.java:215)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReader.getClassReader(SimpleMetadataReader.java:54)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReader.<init>(SimpleMetadataReader.java:48)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.getMetadataReader(SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.java:103)
org.springframework.boot.type.classreading.ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.createMetadataReader(ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.java:86)
org.springframework.boot.type.classreading.ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.getMetadataReader(ConcurrentReferenceCachingMetadataReaderFactory.java:73)
org.springframework.core.type.classreading.SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.getMetadataReader(SimpleMetadataReaderFactory.java:81)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.asSourceClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:630)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser$SourceClass.getInterfaces(ConfigurationClassParser.java:956)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processInterfaces(ConfigurationClassParser.java:396)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.doProcessConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:342)
org.springframework.context.annotation.ConfigurationClassParser.processConfigurationClass(ConfigurationClassParser.java:246)
... 25 common frames omitted
Exit status 1
 

When I encountered this error, I also upgraded com.sap.cloud.mt > orm version to 3.3.5 (latest).

I am still facing the same issue.

Any help on this would be appreciated.

entry.title: Re: How to use SAP GUI Scripting inside Python Programming Language
entry.content:
Hi @maplecui,

RoboSAPiens is designed to make it easy to translate what you would by hand into Python code. If possible avoid translating VBS into Python. It is much easier, since the VBS code contains lots of details which are not relevant for RoboSAPiens. If possible, post an example so that we can help you better.

VS Code can help with autocompletion and inline documentation 🙂

entry.title: Re: Request for SAP S/4HANA Plant Maintenance Fiori Demo...
entry.content:
Greetings,

please refer to online process navigator. Some relevant scenarios in the Acquire to Decommission process are:

  • 4HH - Reactive Maintenance
  • 4HI - Proactive Maintenance
  • 43R - Maintenance Resource Scheduling
  • BH1 - Corrective Maintenance
  • BH2 - Emergency Maintenance
  • BJ2 - Preventive Maintenance - Performance Based

https://me.sap.com/processnavigator

Best regards, M.

entry.content: Hello Victor, Its not possible to change the company code currency. Refer to the additional currency options I have posted above.

entry.title: Re: ArchiveLink connection issue: ERROR => NiBufIConnect:...
entry.content: No, not yet.

entry.title: Re: Select Query Logic For Driver Program For Smart Forms
entry.content:
Don't reinvent the wheel, you could

  • Look at FMs like SD_SALES_DOCUMENT_READ or SD_SALES_DOCUMENT_READ_POS.
    Display a sales order with VA03 and use F1 to get technical info on the field displayed
  • Check for a standard report (smartforms and sap script) in your system
  • Run SQVI and look at the suggested join
  • Do some where-used of this table in SE11 Look for view
  • etc.

entry.title: RFQ Output Status Stuck at 'To be Output' in Manage RFQ Fiori App
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am reaching out for help regarding an issue I encountered in the Manage RFQ Fiori app while processing a Request for Quotation (RFQ). If anyone has faced a similar situation or has insights on how to resolve it, your input would be greatly appreciated.

Issue Description

After following the standard process to create and publish an RFQ, I noticed that the Output Details status is not updating as expected. Here are the steps I followed:

  1. Created an RFQ in the Manage RFQ app.

  2. Published the RFQ successfully.

  3. Observed the Output Details: Instead of showing "Completed," the status remains as "To be Output."

Expected Behavior

Once the RFQ is published, the Output Details status should change to "Completed" if the process has executed successfully.

Possible Causes (Assumptions)

I suspect that one of the following might be causing the issue:

  • Check OPD Transaction: Verify that all configurations in OPD (Output Parameter Determination) are correctly set up and assigned to the appropriate output types.

Request for Help

Has anyone encountered a similar issue in Manage RFQ? If so, what steps did you take to resolve it?

Any guidance or troubleshooting steps would be greatly appreciated! Looking forward to your expert insights.

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Guided Implementation in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content:
Managing an SAP implementation project can be complex, with multiple tasks, dependencies, and stakeholders involved. SAP Guided Implementation in SAP Cloud ALM simplifies this process by providing a structured, role-based approach to project execution. It ensures that project teams focus only on the tasks assigned to their role or directly to them, eliminating clutter and enhancing productivity

This blog introduces the key capabilities, prerequisites, and features of the application, helping stakeholders understand its value and how it enhances project efficiency.

Who Should Use Guided Implementation?

Guided Implementation is designed for project teams involved in SAP implementations. The application is designed for project managers, team members, and quality gate responsible who need an intuitive way to track and execute implementation tasks efficiently.

Guided Implementation is accessible to various project management roles within SAP Cloud ALM, including:

  • Project Lead – Manages project setup, tasks, and timelines.
  • Project Member – Works on assigned tasks within the project.
  • Quality Gate Responsible – Oversees quality gates and checklists.

If you already have access to Projects and Task Management application and have relevant project roles assigned, then you can start using the Guided Implementation application for your projects.

If you are the first-time user, then you can request the Project Lead to assign relevant project roles so that you can see the tasks relevant for you. In addition, Project Lead can create new project and work on the setting up the project for the team members.

Note: The application only displays tasks assigned to your role or directly to you, ensuring a focused view of relevant activities. This ensure that only relevant tasks are displayed based on a user’s assigned responsibilities.

Accessing Guided Implementation

A new tile called 'Guided Implementation' has been introduced in SAP Cloud ALM. Users can now easily launch the application by selecting this tile, providing quick and seamless access to the implementation tasks.

Welcome Screen: Easy Access to Important Filters

Upon launching Guided Implementation, users are greeted with a Welcome Screen that provides quick access to frequently used filters:

  • Tasks assigned to me and my role
  • Tasks assigned directly to me
  • Quality Gates
  • Navigation to Project Progress Reporting

Users can dismiss the Welcome Screen by selecting “Do not show again” and can bring it back from the setting upon the next launch if needed.

Screen Design Overview

The user-friendly layout consists of:

  • Overview of project tasks assigned to your role and directly to you
  • Task List based on the selected roadmap
  • Task Details, including:
    • Task status
    • Responsible person
    • Planned completion date
    • Task description
    • Resources (Accelerators & References)
    • Navigation options (Previous & Next buttons)

Role-Based Task Visibility : 

Only tasks assigned to your role or directly to you appear in the application, reducing clutter and improving focus. For Example: if you are assigned to the role of Business Analyst in the Project then you will see only those tasks that are relevant to the role.

Task Progress Across Project Phases

Guided Implementation provides a clear progress overview by showing:

  • Total tasks relevant to the user
  • Number of completed tasks per phase
  • Progress status per phase

This visibility helps users track progress and prioritize work effectively and stay aligned with project milestones

Create Projects

As a Project Lead, you can now create projects directly within the Guided Implementation application. This eliminates the need to navigate to Projects and Setup separately before returning to Guided Implementation, reducing the number of clicks required. Additionally, users can inline edit foundational tasks, such as: Project Setup , Defining Timelines . More foundational tasks will be included in future roadmap updates.

Switching Between Projects

Users can seamlessly switch between different projects within the Guided Implementation application. This enables them to:

  • View tasks specific to the selected project
  • Easily navigate between multiple active projects
  • View project-specific progress and progress tracking
  • Return to the exact task they were working on before switching projects, ensuring continuity and preserving context

This feature enhances multi-project management by ensuring users work within the right context at all times.

 

Supported Task Types

The application supports:

  • Roadmap Tasks (SAP Recommended)
  • Manual Tasks (custom-defined activities)
  • Subtasks (breakdown of larger tasks)
  • Quality Gates (milestone validations)

Each task type is indicated with a unique icon for easy identification.

 

Task Relations: Understanding Dependencies

Understanding task relationships helps ensure that dependencies are met before progressing. Now you can get transparency on the relationship between the tasks and the application displays an information message if the predecessor task is not completed before the current task is processed.

For seamless execution, tasks are categorized as:

  • Predecessor Tasks (Waiting Tasks)
  • Current Task
  • Successor Tasks (Blocked Tasks)

Users can:

  • View predecessor tasks (must be completed before the current task).
  • See successor tasks (dependent on the current task).
  • Navigate (to task application) between related tasks.
  • Check task ownership, status, and planned dates for dependencies

Search & Filter Capabilities

  • Easy search for tasks in the tree list.
  • Filter based on important attributes like task type, status, role and responsible person. You can also reset filters easily.
  • Phase-Based Filtering: Click on a phase name to display only the tasks relevant to that specific phase. This helps users focus on the tasks within a particular phase without distractions from other project stages.

Task Status Indicators

Users have quick access to task statuses to understand project progress at a glance. The visual appealing icons not only indicate the status of an individual task but also provides an quick way to visualize the overall status of the relevant tasks.

 

Managing Quality Gate Tasks

For tasks of the Quality Gate type, users can now see the status of checklist items directly within the task details. This visibility is crucial for those responsible for ensuring that quality criteria are met before moving to the next stage of the project. In addition to the checklist item status, users can easily navigate to the checklist in the Task Management Application for more detailed information and updates.

      Checklist Item Statuses:

  • The status of each checklist item will be visible in the task details view.
  • The color-coded indicators provide a quick visual cue for the status of each checklist item, ensuring that users can take immediate action if any checklist items are incomplete or overdue.

      Navigate to Checklist:

  • Users can click on the checklist item directly from the task details, which will open the corresponding checklist in the Task Management Application.
  • This functionality ensures that users can quickly address checklist requirements without leaving the Guided Implementation interface, improving workflow efficiency.

 

 

In Summary, SAP Cloud ALM’s Guided Implementation ensures project teams stay focused, organized, and efficient by providing a structured approach to task execution. With features like role-based task visibility, search and filtering, task dependencies, status indicators, and easy navigation, it simplifies implementation processes and enhances collaboration across project teams.

Start leveraging Guided Implementation today and experience a smarter way to manage SAP projects!

entry.title: Re: Course is not moving to learning history after completion
entry.content: What type of course content or launch method does this course have?

entry.title: Re: How to backup transactional data of SAP BPC 10.1 on S...
entry.content: You may have different options depends on why need to backup and how you would like to restore. One common way is to use Backup and Restore tool(tcode UJBR), check this note: https://me.sap.com/notes/1507397/E

thanks a million! Exactly the setting I was looking for and I would never ever have thought about it... you saved my day !

Best regards

Axel

entry.title: Re: ArchiveLink connection issue: ERROR => NiBufIConnect:...
entry.content: Did you already look at a central KBA such as 2582112 - ERROR => NiBufIConnect: non-buffered connect pending after <timeout >ms or raise a case to support?

entry.title: Re: How to use the new GRC Ruleset Manager (GRAC_RULE_SET_MANAGER Sample Excel File)
entry.content: It is very detailed article. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP Lessons Learned: Run a CAP app using SQLite on SAP BTP - Cloud Foundry
entry.content: Excellent @LucasMagriniRigo

entry.title: Re: Joule Setup / Error during Booster Execution
entry.content:
Hi Lukas,

Glad to hear it worked.

This whole Joule setup hinges around Trust or SSO setup, for example, you have to use the same IAS. Putting everything under the same sub-account at least makes life easier. 

Regards,

George

entry.title: Re: I have run cj88 with no errors for a project with actual costs but no change has been made .
entry.content: There are many factors on why nothing was settled. Try running it for the whole project.

entry.title: Re: LMS purge users from success factors and external users
entry.content: generally users on sites are external learning users only and have a different person GUID than internal users. Your internal purging for Sf Platfomr & Learnign users using DRTM tools will be different than th eLearning-only site external users. For the Latter you will want to review the Help Guide links under the LMS > System Administration > Automatic processes > Native User Deletion & native User Data retention along wiht the Purge Deleted User Audit History depending on your scenario.

entry.title: Re: SAP IAG Access Control Mail Notification SMTP Configuration using Onprem Exchange Mail Server
entry.content: It opens new doors to cloud platform and shows the future will form by BTP process.

entry.title: Re: How to turn off the delete function in VA02?
entry.content: Thank you. Article explains the critical point that we always counter the request within the business process.

entry.title: Re: How to add a new field to the VL06O,VL06I, VL06G… screens?
entry.content: Thank you for useful information.

entry.title: Re: ArchiveLink connection issue: ERROR => NiBufIConnect:...
entry.content:
Do you have a firewall (FW) between your SAP system and the archive server?
If yes, please check the firewall logs to see if there are any reasons for session rejections.
Additionally, try extending the icm/conn_timeout parameter from 5 seconds (default) to 15 seconds. This is a dynamic parameter, so only the ICM needs to be restarted.

entry.title: Re: How to add a new field to Business Volume Selection Criteria?
entry.content: It is really helpful when designing the condition contract management process. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SD Dynamic Product Proposal
entry.content: Thank you for very detailed article.

entry.title: Re: SAP SD Variant Pricing: Configuration and Implementation
entry.content: Variant pricing is really key point for variant configuration design. Thank you.

entry.title: Re: Cannot log into AI Launchpad
entry.content: Hi @eshrinivasan, I have added in the requested screenshots for you

entry.title: Re: sap gui installation issue
entry.content: No, Microsoft Office products are not required for SAP GUI installation.

entry.title: Re: CR reports cannot connect to SQL Server database
entry.content:
Does it connect in Crystal Reports Designer?

What version of Cr for Vs are you using?

Look in the Windows Update history to see what MS may have installed and broke your connection.

entry.title: Re: Defining Selection Variants for Shipments
entry.content: Thank you for great article.

entry.title: Re: No transaction type assigned to bank acccount ING 0009156313
entry.content: Please refer Note - 0003517704

entry.title: Re: HTML validation error in User Settings of LMS
entry.content:
You may want to revert to the default first before making changes (remove quotes):
"<p><span class="InstructionalText">To sign in, enter your user ID and password in the space provided to the right and click the Sign In button. If you have forgotten your user ID or password, links below the sign in box will assist you in recovering your credentials. If you do not have an ID and would like to register, please click the "New User?" link below the sign in box.</span></p><p> </p><p> </p>"

entry.title: Re: I have run cj88 with no errors for a project with actual costs but no change has been made .
entry.content: @Ken I am getting the same results when I settle the whole project

entry.title: 'Do you require knowledge for SAP authorization concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Exploring the Authorization Concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite learning journey.

Overview
Learn the fundamental knowledge of the authorization concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite to secure application-level access to your SAP environments (Private Cloud and On-Premise). The role maintenance, as well as details about the elements, strategies, and tools of the authorization concept will be covered.

Learning objectives
This course will prepare you to:

  • Learn about the elements, strategies, and tools of the SAP authorization concept for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite.
  • Understand the terminology of authorizations for SAP S/4HANA and SAP Business Suite.
  • Create and assign authorizations using the Role Maintenance
  • Learn about the basic settings of the Role Maintenance

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area. 

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Return Stock Transfer Order Process
entry.content:
Hi，

When I  /SCWM/PRDO the outbound, the inbound not creat ,error:

 

May I ask if you have encountered it？

How to solve?

entry.title: Re: Cannot log into AI Launchpad
entry.content:
I had to re-establish trust to both Identity providers (just doing the custom/default IAS didnt work).

Thank you to @eshrinivasan for the bit of help leading me to getting there.

entry.title: Re: BAS Application Modeler broken with Fiori tools 1.17.1
entry.content:
Hello All,

I'm also facing this issue, any solution so far?

entry.title: "No Physical Path is configured for Logical File name EHS_FTAPPL_2" CG3Y & CG3Z
entry.content:
Transactions CG3Y & CG3Z are not working.

Whenever we want to download files from any of the Directory in AL11 to Desktop, It is giving Error: "No Physical Path is configured for Logical File name EHS_FTAPPL_2".

I followed already through the following SAP notes, however it does not fixed this scenario:

1949906 - Download and upload of EHS files
3146549 - Information message about the use of transactions CG3Y and CG3Z

Another Important observation is that on a similar environment the physical path "EHS_FTAPPL_PATH_2" was not maintained and it is working.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: "No Physical Path is configured for Logical File name...
entry.content:
Logical file names may be configured with empty Physical file names or use Logical file path without configured Physical file path. Such Logical file name can pass directory traversal validation process (performed by FM FILE_VALIDATE_NAME) with arbitrary Physical file name. In such a case only security audit log is written but actually no directory traversal validation is performed (validation always passes). It can be considered as security issue.

For this reason the validation enforcement switch has been introduced. It enforces file validation error (message SG813) in case the Logical file name has no or empty configuration. This switch can be maintained in transaction SM30 for Table/View FILECMCUST. The parameter name is REJECT_EMPTY_PATH and it can be switched on with value 'ON' or off with any other value.

For this scenario, you have two possible solutions:

Solution 1) Configure the Logical file name. It can be done in transaction FILE. To configure the logical file name for "EHS_FTAPPL_2", please follow the instructions provided in the following KBA:

0003040953 - Error "No physical path is configured for logical file name EHS_FTAPPL_2" occurs in transactions CG3Y/CG3Z

In case you need help on how to fulfill the physical path for "EHS_FTAPPL_2", Please refer to the example at the end of SAP note " 2887937 - EHS: Potential Directory Traversal".

Solution 2) if security is not an issue, the validation enforcement can be switched off. Open transaction "SM30" for Table/View "FILECMCUST" and set the value of parameter "REJECT_EMPTY_PATH" to "OFF". Be aware that this is considered as a serious security risk because it switches the validation off also for all other unconfigured Logical File Names.

For further information, please refer to the following SAP note:

2395138 - Validation enforcement for empty Logical file name configuration

Also, please note that we only support "CG3Z" & "CG3Y" if it relates to EHS Files. Please check SAP Note " 1949906 - Download and upload of files and SAP Note".

Best regards,
Felipe

entry.title: Re: Early billing in Phased Contract in BRIM
entry.content:
Hi,

Date type EARLYBILDATE (Early Billing Date) is delivered to be used for the scenario I explained in my reply. It is part of standard date profile PROVITEM since S/4HANA 2023 FPS2 release. Thanks.

Regards
Deepak

entry.title: Re: Can I perform SD integration for utility products without using AVC?

Hi @Hazhir,

did you find a solution?

Kind regards

Till

entry.title: Re: Release API-State Error "Retrieved API version of object is inconsistent" RAP, BTP
entry.content: Hi @sebastian_wilhelm1, did you manage to solve this problem?

entry.title: Re: Encapsulation of Crystal Reports 8.5 library in Windo...
entry.content:
Time to upgrade to .NET.

The legacy RDC engine is COM based and likely problem is the dependencies are missing.

That component hasn't shipped since CR 10 days. The reason is Microsoft decided .NET Framework was going to replace COM so SAP decided to drop the RDC for CR .NET dependencies, Of course too many people complained so MS changed their mind and supported both again. But it was to late, CR dropped the craxdrt.dll.

The other issue is MS stopped supporting programs from installing into the c:\windows\system32 folder where CR installed into c:\windows\system32\crystal folder. So it's likely missing as well.

The bottom line is using that legacy COM dll is no longer supported by CR and Windows.

You could rewrite your dll as a service using the CR .NET references and continue opening/exporting to PDF.

CR is also recommending to move to 64 bit also, 32 bit references will no longer be created end of 2025.

The reason your app is attempting to open the report using the GUI s because it can't find the RDC dll.

To upgrade go here for references:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_CRYSTAL_REPORTS,_DEVELOPER_VERSION_FOR_MICROSOFT_VISUAL_STUDIO?locale=en-US

And here to download and other material including .NET samples:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SUPPORT_CONTENT/crystalreports/3354091173.html

Related Documents

Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Licensing

Getting started and moving ahead with Crystal Reports .NET applications

See this Blog for instructions upgrading your existing project to the latest SP

See this blog on how to use CR 2020 Export to XLXS page formats:

How to use the new CR 2020 export to Microsoft Excel(XLXS) in Visual Studio .NET code (new)

how-to-parameters-in-crystal-reports-for-visual-studio-net

Printing Crystal Reports in .NET

SAP Crystal Reports for Visual Studio Installation Guide ( SP 21 and above)

Rather than spend time and resources on trying to make the legacy app to work I highly recommend you get a developer to rewrite your Windows Service using the current .NET SDK. That legacy COM dll requires Windows dependencies that Microsoft no longer installs/deploys.

entry.title: how to set a filter "field = empty " in FIORI App manage KPIs and reports
entry.content:
hi there,

when I create a KPI using the Fiori App ‘Manage KPIs and Reports’, I want to set a filter value, that means DELETION INDICATOR = ' '. I already used "#" and <> 'X', but the result is always wrong.

Another try is to select Values like ORDER NUMBER like "001%" or "001*", meaning all orders tat start with 001.

entry.title: Re: Error while using XML to CSV Out of the box SAP Solut...
entry.content:
Issue is resolved.  For each structure code is expecting keyFieldValue, KeyFieldName, missingLastFields are required here.  After adding these additional parameters conversion happening correctly.

=================================================================

xml.keyFieldName = RecordType
xml.<recordstructurename>.missingLastFields : add
xml.Oildex_Electronic.keyFieldValue = 7
xml.Oildex_Electronic_Summary.keyFieldValue = 8
xml.Oildex_Electronic_Tran.keyFieldValue = 9

entry.title: Re: Release API-State Error "Retrieved API version of object is inconsistent" RAP, BTP
entry.content: No, unfortunately not!

entry.title: Re: Dynamically embed PDF based on path provided by repor...
entry.content: That is the way Comment works, not sure why SAP did it this way???? Not very good user interface for commenting, you have to enter somethign andf then edit it....

entry.title: MDG. How to turn off auto replication of materials
entry.content:
Dear SAP community,

Currently we have the following situation: When changing a material in MM02 or using MASS, IDOCs are automatically created and replication is performed. For change requests, the replication step is not added in BRF+, but replication is still performed automatically. If I add a replication step in BRF+, it is executed twice when the request is activated (two IDOCs are created with a time difference of less than a second for one material). How can I disable automatic replication, which is triggered by changes in MM02 or MASS? A similar question has already been raised, but the solution was not written: SAP MDG auto-replicating material to ECC via MM02 ... - SAP Community

entry.title: Pricing is not correctly determined for Sales Orders When Fast Entering Line Items
entry.content:
When entering items on a Sales Order document without selecting the product from the product list dropdown, or without hitting the Enter button after entering some data, the pricing might not be determined correctly.

In general, this practice is performed to enter the items faster on a Sales Order, specially when there are too many products.

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Pricing is not correctly determined for Sales Orders...
entry.content:
The Fast Entry functionality should be used in these cases according to the following steps:

  1. Open the Sales Order.
  2. Go to the Items tab.
  3. Click on Fast Entry. Ten empty lines will be created.
  4. Add multiple items (you can enter them any way you want, hitting the Enter button is not necessary).
  5. After the item entries are completed, Click on the Check Fast Entry button.

The system then checks all data created and performs pricing and product availability. This way, the pricing will always be calculated correctly, and it is a good option to enter multiple items quickly, but still consistently.

For more information on the Fast Entry functionality, please check the Help Portal Sales Orders Quick Guide - Create a Sales Order (section no. 7, item C, there is a note describing the Fast Entry)

Best Regards,

Marcelo
SAP Product Support

entry.title: Re: Dynamically embed PDF based on path provided by repor...
